message:
feat(assign/effectiveness): rank against all supported agent types

`EffectivenessIntegrator.RankAgentsForTask` previously only
walked the three original agent types — `AgentClaude`,
`AgentCodex`, `AgentGemini` — and returned a ranked list that
silently excluded the four newer providers the rest of the
codebase already supports: `AgentCursor`, `AgentWindsurf`,
`AgentAider`, and `AgentOllama`.

This meant any code that routed work via
`RankAgentsForTask(taskType)` and then dispatched to the
top-scoring agent would never dispatch to any of those four,
even when their base effectiveness scores against the current
task were higher. Users running swarms with Cursor / Windsurf /
Aider / Ollama enabled were effectively getting a three-agent
scheduler with four invisible ones.

Extend the hardcoded `agentTypes` slice in the same order the
agent registry uses:

    []tmux.AgentType{
        tmux.AgentClaude, tmux.AgentCodex, tmux.AgentGemini,
        tmux.AgentCursor, tmux.AgentWindsurf, tmux.AgentAider,
        tmux.AgentOllama,
    }

The rest of the ranking loop — calling
`ei.matrix.GetScore(agent, task)` and emitting an
`EffectivenessRanking` entry per agent — is unchanged, so the
seven agents now compete on the same scale against the current
task.
